threat.title: Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF) in Express.js REST API
threat.description: When REST APIs accept state-changing requests without CSRF tokens, attackers can trick authenticated users into executing unwanted actions by embedding malicious requests in third-party websites.

attack.scenario:
1. User logs into vulnerable web application
2. Attacker sends user a link to malicious website
3. Malicious site contains hidden form that auto-submits to victim's bank
4. Request includes user's session cookie (sent automatically by browser)
5. Application accepts request without CSRF validation
6. Unauthorized action executed (e.g., transfer money, change password)
prerequisites: State-changing endpoints (POST, PUT, DELETE) lack CSRF protection
User is authenticated and has active session
Attacker can trick user into visiting malicious site

real_world_breaches.impact: Attackers could add/delete videos from user accounts
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Missing CSRF tokens on state-changing actions

bug_bounty_reports.summary: CSRF on password change endpoint allowing account takeover

security_research.title: OWASP CSRF Prevention Cheat Sheet
security_research.key_finding: CSRF affects 70% of web applications without proper token validation

mitigations.title: Implement CSRF Tokens with csurf Middleware
mitigations.description: Use synchronizer token pattern to validate that requests originate from your application.
nist_csf: PR.AC-1 - Identities and credentials are managed
validation_steps: Add csurf middleware to Express app
Include CSRF token in all forms and AJAX requests
Test POST/PUT/DELETE without token (should fail with 403)

mitigations.title: Use SameSite Cookie Attribute
mitigations.description: Set SameSite=Strict or SameSite=Lax on session cookies to prevent cross-site request attacks.
validation_steps: Set SameSite attribute on all session cookies
Test cross-origin requests (should not include cookies)
Verify cookie headers in browser DevTools

mitigations.title: Validate Origin and Referer Headers
mitigations.description: Check that requests originate from your own domain (defense in depth).
validation_steps: Add Origin/Referer validation to POST/PUT/DELETE routes
Test with requests from unauthorized origins (should fail)

indicators: State-changing requests with missing or invalid CSRF tokens
Session cookies sent on cross-origin requests

log_queries.query:
// Detect CSRF attempts
AppServiceHTTPLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
| where CsMethod in ('POST', 'PUT', 'DELETE')
| where CsReferer !startswith 'https://yourdomain.com'
| summarize AttemptCount = count() by ClientIP, CsUriStem, CsReferer
| where AttemptCount > 10
| order by AttemptCount desc

tools.name: Burp Suite
tools.usage: Generate CSRF PoC and test endpoints
